key_insight: MCL-PINNs combines phase-field physics with specialized neural training tricks to accurately solve moving contact line problems with dynamic boundary conditions.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a mesh-free way to simulate wetting, droplet motion, and interfacial evolution in complex fluid systems where standard PINNs struggle.
method: Discrete-time PINN with multi-network time marching
Relaxed output distribution plus variable scaling for sharp interfaces
Adaptive loss weighting and adaptive collocation sampling with interface extraction
evidence_signal: The method is validated on three numerical examples—droplet coalescence, shear-induced deformation, and heterogeneous-channel wetting—and is reported to outperform standard PINNs in accuracy and robustness.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful training design, including adaptive sampling and scaling, so implementation may be sensitive and more complex than a vanilla PINN.
paper_title: A phase-field neural solver for moving contact line problems with dynamic boundary conditions
